titleStmt.title: Letter from A. F. Falkiner to Sir Robert Chalmers, 8 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: A.F. Falkiner

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from the Governor of Limerick Prison, A. F. Falkiner, to Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938), Under-Secretary for Ireland, confirming the information in a telegram. He regrets the 12-day delay in enacting relief from a section of Richard Carroll's bail sentence. He also regrets the delay in acknowledging the order. Carroll had been sentenced to six months' hard labour for assaulting his wife. The order is included as an enclosure.Sir Robert Chalmers was appointed Under-Secretary of Ireland in 1916, replacing Sir Matthew Nathan in the aftermath of the Easter Rising. Chalmers refused compensation for civilians killed and wounded in the Easter Rising and was reportedly disdainful toward the people of Ireland.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
H. M. Prison Limerick 8th June 1916 Sir, In confirmation of telegram
sent you this evening I beg to
report that the order conveying
the desire of Their Excellencies the
Lords Justices that the position
of the sentence imposed on prisoner
Richard Carroll 130/16, relating
to bail, be not acted on, was
received on the 27th and
will be compiled with. It is very much regretted
that through an oversight the
acknowledgment was not despatched
on same date. I have the honor to be
Sir
your obdt. servant A.F.Falkiner.
Governor. The UnderSecretary &c .&c Dublin Castle I letter in
communication
back
FORM 33A. C26 1916
Application for Discharge of a Prisoner
County of...Tipperary
Name of Prisoner,...Richard Carroll 130/16 Limik
Age on Conviction...24 years
Before whom tried...Templemore P.S.
Whether pleaded Guilty or not
Guilty? â
Offence,...Aggravated assault on wife
Sentence,... br.m. hl. bail or 3r. mor.
Date of Conviction,...5:4:16
Date from which Sentence takes effect
Previous Character convictions ...two.
Eligible for release, if well conducted
in meantime,...5:9:16 The Solicitor General advises
that the order for imprisonment in default of
bail should not be acted on, and your
Excellency's directions are sought accordingly. As regards the memorial for
reduction of the sentence of b.c. men. hl. this
was a similarly savage assault and the
magistrates regret that it was not possible
to inflict a heavier penalty. Submitted 18 23 :5:16 Russell Bailey 24.5.16 Where sent to
governor as to
acknowledgement
W 8/6
